1: And Balaam said unto Balak, Build me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven oxen and seven rams.
2: And Balak did as Balaam had spoken; and Balak and Balaam offered on every every altar a bullock and a ram.
3: And Balaam said unto Balak, Stand by thy burnt offering, and I will go: peradventure the LORD LORD will come to meet me: and whatsoever he sheweth me I will tell thee. And he went to to an high place.
4: And God met Balaam: and he said unto him, I have prepared seven altars, and I have offered upon every every altar a bullock and a ram.
5: And the LORD LORD put a word in Balaam's mouth, and said, Return unto Balak, and thus thou shalt speak.
6: And he returned unto him, and, lo, he stood by his burnt sacrifice, he, and all the princes of Moab.
8: Or how shall I defy, whom whom the LORD LORD hath not defied?
9: And shall not be reckoned among the nations.
10: And let my last end be like his!
11: And Balak said unto Balaam, What hast thou done unto me? I took thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast blessed them them altogether.
12: And he answered and said, Must I not take heed to speak that which the LORD LORD hath put in my mouth?
13: And Balak said unto him, Come, I pray thee, with me unto another place, from whence thou mayest see them: thou shalt see but the utmost part of them, and shalt not see them all: and curse me them from thence.
14: And he brought him into the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah, and built seven altars, and offered a bullock and a ram on every every altar.
15: And he said unto Balak, Stand here by thy burnt offering, while I meet the LORD the LORD LORD yonder.
16: And the LORD LORD met Balaam, and put a word in his mouth, and said, Go again unto Balak, and say thus.
17: And when he came to him, behold, he stood by his burnt offering, and the princes of Moab with him. And Balak said unto him, What hath the LORD LORD spoken?
18: Hearken unto me, thou son of Zippor:
19: Or hath he spoken, and shall he not make it good?
20: And he hath blessed; and I cannot reverse it.
21: And the shout of a king is is among them.
22: He hath as it were the strength of an unicorn.
24: And drink the blood of the slain.
25: And Balak said unto Balaam, Neither curse them at all, nor bless them at all.
26: But Balaam answered and said unto Balak, Told not I thee, saying, All that the LORD LORD speaketh, that I must do?
27: And Balak said unto Balaam, Come, I pray thee, I will bring thee unto another place; peradventure it will please God that thou mayest curse me them from thence.
28: And Balak brought Balaam unto the top of Peor, that looketh toward Jeshimon.
29: And Balaam said unto Balak, Build me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven bullocks and seven rams.
30: And Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered a bullock and a ram on every every altar.
